name: Castillo de Boixadors - loop in the Castelltallat Sierra Protected Natural Area
name_original: Castillo de Boixadors - bucle en el Espacio Natural Protegido de la Sierra de Castelltallat
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around La Molsosa are found in the southernmost part of the Solsonès region, within the natural area of Alta Segarra. The landscape features a mix of flatlands and gentle mountainous elevations, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Routes traverse scenic streams, extensive forests, and cultivated fields, often utilizing quiet secondary roads and rural paths. This area provides a tranquil rural setting for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around La Molsosa The most popular touring cycling route is Castillo de Boixadors - loop in the Castelltallat Sierra Protected Natural Area, a difficult 22.9 miles (36.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route explores the protected natural area, offering varied terrain and views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mare de la Font Park – Sanctuary of El Miracle loop from Monestir de Cellers, a difficult 72.3 miles (116.3 km) path. This route connects natural parkland with a significant sanctuary, showcasing diverse scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Ruins of the Calaf castle – Convent de Sant Francesc loop from Calaf, a 33.7 miles (54.2 km) trail leading through historical sites, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Touring cycling around La Molsosa is defined by serene rural roads, diverse natural scenery, and historical and cultural sites. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in La Molsosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from a few reviews. More than 280 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore La Molsosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around La Molsosa?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around La Molsosa, offering a variety of experiences. Most routes are classified as difficult (39), with some moderate (12) and a few easy (1) options, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the La Molsosa area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974493115/castillo-de-boixadors-loop-in-the-castelltallat-sierra-protected-natural-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Boixadors - loop in the Castelltallat Sierra Protected Natural Area&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 22.9-mile route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31856395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mare de la Font Park – Sanctuary of El Miracle loop from Monestir de Cellers&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 72.3-mile difficult path.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore while cycling around La Molsosa?
A: The region is rich in history and culture. You can cycle through medieval villages like Prades, els Quadrells, and Enfesta. Notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3246951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Villa of Sant Amanç de Viladés&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5069759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Pinós&lt;/a&gt;, which offers magnificent panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5070943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelltallat Castle&lt;/a&gt;, housing an astronomical observatory, is also a unique landmark.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on touring cycling routes near La Molsosa?
A: The landscape around La Molsosa is diverse, featuring a mix of flatlands and gentle mountainous elevations. You'll traverse scenic streams, extensive forests, and cultivated fields, often utilizing quiet secondary roads and rural paths. This provides a tranquil rural setting with varied terrain.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by castles or historical ruins?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical castles and ruins. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20080134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Calaf castle – Convent de Sant Francesc loop from Calaf&lt;/a&gt; is a 33.7-mile trail leading through historical sites. You can also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5095529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Boixadors&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5070943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelltallat Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about cycling in La Molsosa?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Reviewers often praise the serene rural roads, diverse natural scenery, and the opportunity to explore historical and cultural sites away from heavy traffic.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: While most routes are classified as difficult, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them suitable for those new to touring cycling or families looking for a relaxed ride. Always check the route details for specific difficulty and terrain.

Q: What is the typical duration for touring cycling routes in this region?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, a difficult 22.9-mile route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974493115/castillo-de-boixadors-loop-in-the-castelltallat-sierra-protected-natural-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Boixadors loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours and 9 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the 72.3-mile &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31856395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mare de la Font Park – Sanctuary of El Miracle loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 8 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant elevation changes for a challenging ride?
A: Yes, many routes in the La Molsosa area feature significant elevation changes, providing a challenging experience for touring cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31856395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mare de la Font Park – Sanctuary of El Miracle loop&lt;/a&gt; includes nearly 2000 meters of elevation gain, making it a demanding ride through varied terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that connect natural parks with significant landmarks?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31856395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mare de la Font Park – Sanctuary of El Miracle loop from Monestir de Cellers&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, connecting natural parkland with the significant Sanctuary of El Miracle, showcasing diverse scenery and cultural importance.

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in La Molsosa?
A: Given the region's diverse landscapes and tranquil rural setting, spring and autumn are generally ideal for touring cycling. The weather is typically milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant or colorful. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring cooler temperatures, so checking local forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there any routes that start from or pass through the Castelltallat mountain range?
A: Yes, the Castelltallat mountain range is a prominent feature in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974493115/castillo-de-boixadors-loop-in-the-castelltallat-sierra-protected-natural-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Boixadors - loop in the Castelltallat Sierra Protected Natural Area&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores this protected natural area, offering varied terrain and views, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5070943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelltallat Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

http://www.altaanoia.info/es/Activitats/Visita-guiada-al-Castell-de-Boixadors

The Castell de Boixadors is on top of a hill in a very privileged position. It is located at the western end of the Castelltallat mountain range. It was important in times of reconquest. It has been documented since 1015. Assigned to the Salavinera City Council, since 1971 the Barcelona Provincial Council has carried out several restoration campaigns.

Located beside the local road BV-3003. The place is well marked with signs.

An astronomical observatory inaugurated in 2004, is located in the Sierra de Castelltallat in the municipality of Sant Mateu de Bages on a hill at 911 meters above sea level, where there are also the ruins of the castle of Montedono which has its origins in the seventh century.

The Serra de Castelltallat is a very depopulated place, there is only the presence of some farmhouses that make very little light at night, the situation of the Observatory, a thousand meters high, and isolated from any town, makes become a privileged place for sky observation.

It offers activities designed for all types of audiences. Website: https://astrocastellblog.wordpress.com/
